item.title: Re: Images
item.content:encoded:
Actually, the above link leads to incorrect image. Correct image is as follows.

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0

Title: File:Namaqua Chamäleon-01.jpg
Author: Hans Stieglitz
Uploaded on: 2012

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mildly disappointed that the result of the gun test wasn't a gun that shoots out a chameleon tongue instead of bullets. But the article is still good enough for a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was literally just about to post "Karma karma karma karma chameleon!" but you beat me to it. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you treat her badly, it comes back to you. I'm guessing in that sense it's a karma chameleon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Technological devices affected by SCP-2186 appear to retain some rudimentary functionality. Further testing is necessary in this area.

Oh, no. They're gonna do it, aren't they. They're gonna-

Test Object: 9-millimeter handgun

They did it.
+1

item.title: @Khym Chanur
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but I do believe it's more fun this way!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Note the testing area's adoption of a similar pattern and texture to SCP-2186's scales.

This doesn't seem to accurately describe the current image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've added just such a thing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd imagine that since SCP-2186 is immune to her own anomalous effects, her hypothetical offspring would be as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see a test with a non-anomalous chameleon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clever, well-written, amusing in a creepyish way. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but if she gets nervous, will the fertilized eggs be transformed into clones of her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Reverse Chameleon" is a much better name for this than "Chameleonscaper", by the way.

The name did occur to me, but I felt it was too on-the-nose.

The malnutrition and vitamin deficiency - those are because everything the chameleon comes in contact with is just more of herself.

Not necessarily so. The SCP only transmutes its surroundings when in an agitated state; when the SCP is content and happy, its surroundings - and therefore, its food - remain normal. The SCP was in poor health upon discovery because it was being so poorly taken care of. I'm updating the entry as we speak to make this more clear.

What happens if she lays eggs? Will they all be female?

To my knowledge, female chameleons can't bear offspring from unfertilized eggs (although some lizards can). The SCP would presumably be able to mate with a male of the same species, and lay eggs that would hatch into a clutch of mixed-sex offspring.

item.title: Re: Images
item.content:encoded: Oh, yeah. Sorry about that. ;nn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome inversion taken to its logical extreme, with a nice bit of foreshadowing in the opening. I love it.

+1

item.title: Images
item.content:encoded:
For the record, the image in this article is taken from the Wikimedia Commons —

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Laika_ac_Namaqua_Chameleon_%288446604360%29.jpg

and is available as per Creative Commons. Original photographer is Flickr user "Laika ac".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure the being-rendered-unusable is just incidental. A chameleon doesn't understand what a gun is, and I doubt it would be able to conceive of a difference between a knife and a stick.

"Reverse Chameleon" is a much better name for this than "Chameleonscaper", by the way.

The malnutrition and vitamin deficiency - those are because everything the chameleon comes in contact with is just more of herself.

What happens if she lays eggs? Will they all be female?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great, although I don't know that the test logs or discovery thing are necessary. Nonetheless, an enjoyable old-school vibe from this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I shall plus this one with a one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure how I feel about that last log, but I do appreciate that they stopped testing on the chameleon itself after a while. Overall, a fun object with a neat effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoyed the concept and the fridge horror of its owner being alive but permanently changed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reverse chameleon.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing like a nice, well-executed thing-what-does-a-thing. There's always space for the classics. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You did improve on it, as expected of you. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the implication that if exposed to a potentially threatening object (Like the handgun), it transmutes it in such way its rendered useless, I suspect that if exposed to, lest say, a knife, it will render it dull for example.

Overall, nice skip. +1

Block 21 is complete again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm quite pleased that each test has a clearly defined purpose, and that the Foundation decided not to continue testing the chameleon's effect on people. Glad to see that people understand what a test log should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An intriguing premise, with an enjoyable test log. Some of the description is kind of clunky in how the stages of affliction are described but that didn't detract from my enjoyment. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The changes you've suggested have been implemented. Thanks for your feedback - and for your appreciation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the bolding of the transformation stages is necessary, and I think the test logs would greatly benefit from being in quote boxes (they're kind of a mess to look at), but all-in-all this is awesome.
